note: scanIndex (cmd/heurema-hygiene/main.go) now reads staged blob content via a single `git cat-file --batch` over all index paths instead of os.ReadFile on worktree bytes, so the gate verifies exactly what a commit would record. Pinned by TestScanIndexReadsStagedContent (staged secret flagged after worktree scrub; unstaged worktree edit not scanned).

Same change as f_006: content now comes from index blobs (`:path` objects via cat-file --batch), eliminating the index-selection/worktree-content mismatch. Verified by tests and an end-to-end smoke where staged content diverging from the worktree is caught with redacted previews and exit 1.

The silent fs.ErrNotExist skip is gone: files are read from the index, so worktree deletion is irrelevant, and readBatchBlob treats any non-blob cat-file response (e.g. 'missing') as a hard error rather than a skip. Covered by TestScanIndexReadsStagedContent (staged-added file with deleted worktree copy is flagged) plus a binary-built smoke run reproducing the exact scenario (exit 1).
